$comment: Draft schema. Not finalized — structure may change.
title: FedRAMP Incident Report (IEC-CSO-IIR / IEC-CSO-OIR / IEC-CSO-FIR)
description: Unified schema for the three incident report types in the IEC-CSO lifecycle: Initial (IEC-CSO-IIR), Ongoing (IEC-CSO-OIR), and Final (IEC-CSO-FIR). Reports are linked by providerTrackingId. Notify fedramp_security@fedramp.gov, agency customers, and all necessary parties per the notification requirements in each rule.

reportType.title: Report Type
reportType.description: Initial = IEC-CSO-IIR; Ongoing = IEC-CSO-OIR; Final = IEC-CSO-FIR.

providerTrackingId.title: Provider Tracking ID
providerTrackingId.description: Provider's internally assigned identifier for this incident. Must be consistent across IIR, OIR, and FIR reports for the same incident.

federalIncidentCoordinator.title: Federal Incident Response Coordinator
federalIncidentCoordinator.description: Contact information for the provider's federal incident response coordinator.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.description: Timeline of the incident per IEC-CSO-IIR field 4.

evaluationCompletedAt.title: FedRAMP Reportable Incident Evaluation Completed At

potentialAgencyImpact.title: Potential Agency Impact
potentialAgencyImpact.description: Historical and current PAIN rating. See IEC-CSO-EFI for rating definitions and IEC-CSO-DPR for the default PAIN-5 assumption when not yet estimated.

evaluationNotes.title: Evaluation Notes
evaluationNotes.description: Explanation of the PAIN evaluation per IEC-CSO-EFI. Required if applicable.

recoveryPlan.title: Recovery Plan
recoveryPlan.description: Estimated recovery plan, milestones, and timelines.

observedActivity.title: Observed Incident Activity
observedActivity.description: Per IEC-CSO-OIR. Include in Ongoing and Final reports.

resolvedAt.title: Resolved At
resolvedAt.description: When the incident was resolved and recovery completed. Required for Final reports (IEC-CSO-FIR).
